The '''Jin'ha''' were a enigmatical [[Species/Legends|species]] famous for their work with the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-resistant [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. The Jin'ha also sold some of their cortosis ore to the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], which utilized it for their experimental [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]]. The common weapons the Jin'ha used were pikes, maces and [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]].

==History==
The Jin'ha were a mystic [[Species/Legends|species]] who operated a mine and refinery on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Obredaan]]. The Jin'ha produced weapons from refined [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-resistant [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] that they shipped to various groups such as the [[Black Heth]] and the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. Many were trained as warriors and some as specialized snipers.<ref name="SW:OW" />

The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] fought a group of Jin'ha during a weapons shipment to the [[Black Heth]] criminal gang. Later, he and his [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] [[Second mission to Obredaan|fought]] an army of Jin'ha on Obredaan in order to free the captive Jedi Masters [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] and [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. The Jin'ha proved to be capable warriors during this conflict, but the Jedi proved victorious.<ref name="SW:OW" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Jin'ha first appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']].
